questions.q: The process by which the ocean water flows from the equatorial region towards the poles is called:
Describe the role of trade winds in the formation of ocean currents.
questions.answer: Trade winds play a crucial role in the formation of ocean currents. These winds push the surface water in the direction of the wind, creating surface currents. This is an example of wind-driven ocean circulation.

questions.q: What are the factors that influence the movement of ocean water? (Any two)
questions.answer: The factors that influence the movement of ocean water are wind, Coriolis force, and the shape of the ocean floor.

questions.q: The Gulf Stream is an example of a:

questions.answer: Upwelling is the process by which the deep, cold water rises to the surface of the ocean, often as a result of winds pushing the surface water away from the coast.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The Coriolis force causes the ocean water to move in a ______________ direction, resulting in the formation of large-scale ocean circulation patterns.
Describe the process of thermohaline circulation.
questions.answer: Thermohaline circulation is the movement of ocean water driven by changes in temperature and salinity. It occurs when the surface water becomes denser due to cooling or salting, causing it to sink to the bottom of the ocean. This process creates a circulation of water between the surface and the deep sea.

questions.q: What is the difference between a wind-driven ocean current and a thermohaline ocean current?
questions.answer: Wind-driven ocean currents are formed by the action of winds on the surface of the ocean, while thermohaline ocean currents are formed by changes in temperature and salinity.

questions.q: The Agulhas Current is an example of a:
Explain the concept of Ekman transport.
questions.answer: Ekman transport is the movement of ocean water perpendicular to the wind direction, due to the Coriolis force. It occurs when the wind pushes the surface water in one direction, but the ocean water moves in a different direction due to the Coriolis force. This results in a net movement of water perpendicular to the wind direction.
